retiredReason: Cardinal-sin VERIFICATION FAILURE (FAS=24, 1V/1INC/4MIS/8UNV, 7 sins). Addiction-as-medical-condition principle verified. "8,500 waitlist Dec 2025", "52,000/170,000 coverage" CONTRADICTED by UNAIDS (Malaysia >85,000 in OST, reached 70% coverage 2017-2021 — opposite trajectory). France OST dates wrong (since 1996, not 2018). Stage 3 output: engine/output/opioid-substitution-therapy-waitlist-stage3.json. Topic can be covered as a NEW issue with a fresh primary-source brief; this ID is retired.
headline: Opioid Substitution Therapy Waitlist Grows to 8,500 Patients
context: Methadone and buprenorphine treatment programs cannot accommodate growing demand from opioid-dependent patients.

cards.big: 8,500 patients on waitlist for opioid substitution therapy as of December 2025
cards.sub: Only 52,000 of estimated 170,000 opioid users receive any form of treatment

cards.big: Average wait time for methadone program entry is 4.5 months in Klang Valley
cards.sub: Patients waiting for treatment face overdose risk 6 times higher than those in treatment

cards.big: Government methadone clinics operate only during office hours excluding working patients
cards.sub: Rigid scheduling forces patients to choose between treatment and employment

cards.big: France expanded OST through community pharmacies reaching 180,000 patients since 2018
cards.sub: Pharmacy-based distribution dramatically increased treatment accessibility and retention

cards.big: Making people wait months for addiction treatment is sentencing them to continued suffering
Like making diabetics queue 8 months for insulin: the treatment exists, the patients are identified, but the bottleneck is between them
cards.sub: Addiction is a medical condition with effective treatment; the waitlist converts a treatable condition into preventable suffering

cards.big: Should opioid substitution therapy be available through community pharmacies not just clinics
